checks.finding: REQ-004 says Draft/Planned tasks may exist only after predecessor stages “required by their phase” are valid, but neither the phase-to-predecessor matrix nor the meaning of valid for those states is specified, and AC-005 covers only Approved/In Progress/Implementation Complete/Done. A task author or verifier therefore cannot decide which review statuses make a Draft or Planned task conformant. This is Phase 1 requirement and acceptance coverage, not an implementation-detail question. Major is justified because the ambiguity can produce incompatible validators and fixtures, while the remaining lifecycle safety boundary is explicit.

The integrity-enforcement goal is covered by AC-003 through AC-010 and AC-014; behavior preservation by AC-007, AC-010, AC-012, and AC-013; historical-debt handling by AC-006 and AC-011; and deterministic runtime parity by AC-002 and AC-008.
AC-001 through AC-014 use observable exit status, stable rule IDs, rejected or accepted fixtures, invocation-path inspection, traceable records, synchronized versions, and existing-suite outcomes.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude fabricated historical reviews, policy changes, replacement of stage prechecks, installer or uninstaller behavior changes, and new services or runtime dependencies; profile boundaries are also stated in REQ-005 and REQ-006.

The specification states ADR and contract requirements, legacy eligibility, Done authority, synchronized-version constraints, fail-closed behavior, path safety, historical-provenance limits, runtime parity, and preservation of existing approval and evidence gates.

High-risk surfaces have planned validation: provenance forgery and staleness in AC-004, privilege bypass in AC-005, bounded migration exceptions in AC-006, path escape in AC-002, integration enforcement in AC-009 and AC-010, cross-runtime/CRLF parity in AC-008, and fail-closed corruption or read denial in AC-014.
